articles.title: Dollar set for sharp weekly loss versus yen after Japan steps in
articles.content: The dollar logged its sharpest weekly drop against the yen since February amid suspected Japanese intervention. Authorities signalled discomfort with currency volatility, keeping markets cautious. However, sustained yen strength remains uncertain due to policy divergence with the Federal Reserve, while traders watch for further intervention during Japan’s Golden Week holiday period.
articles.excerpt: The dollar logged its sharpest weekly drop against the yen since February amid suspected Japanese intervention. Authorities signalled discomfort with currency volatility, keeping markets cautious. However, sustained yen strength remains uncertain due to policy divergence with the Federal Reserve, while traders watch for further intervention during Japan’s Golden Week holiday period.

articles.title: Gold turns positive as oil eases on hopes for Iran talks
articles.content: Gold edged higher after recovering from sharp early losses, supported by hopes of easing Iran tensions and a softer dollar. However, the metal remained on track for a weekly decline amid a hawkish Federal Reserve stance. Inflation risks and elevated yields continued to cap gains, while silver outperformed on strong demand outlook.
articles.excerpt: Gold edged higher after recovering from sharp early losses, supported by hopes of easing Iran tensions and a softer dollar. However, the metal remained on track for a weekly decline amid a hawkish Federal Reserve stance. Inflation risks and elevated yields continued to cap gains, while silver outperformed on strong demand outlook.

articles.title: Nifty has best month since December 2023; defence, capital markets & realty put the shine on April
articles.content: Indian equities staged a strong rebound in April after months of decline. Benchmark and broader indices posted their best gains in years, led by midcaps and smallcaps, while sectoral indices like defence and realty outperformed. Banks and IT saw moderate recovery.
articles.excerpt: Indian equities staged a strong rebound in April after months of decline. Benchmark and broader indices posted their best gains in years, led by midcaps and smallcaps, while sectoral indices like defence and realty outperformed. Banks and IT saw moderate recovery.

articles.title: Oil falls as Iran proposes talks but prices still set for weekly gains
articles.content: Oil prices dipped amid reports of renewed Iran-US negotiation efforts, but remained on track for strong weekly gains due to persistent supply disruptions. Ongoing tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and geopolitical uncertainty kept markets volatile, with traders reacting sharply to headlines despite a fragile ceasefire and continued export restrictions.
articles.excerpt: Oil prices dipped amid reports of renewed Iran-US negotiation efforts, but remained on track for strong weekly gains due to persistent supply disruptions. Ongoing tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and geopolitical uncertainty kept markets volatile, with traders reacting sharply to headlines despite a fragile ceasefire and continued export restrictions.

articles.title: UK's FTSE 100 slips as losses in energy stocks and AstraZeneca weigh
articles.content: London’s FTSE 100 edged lower as losses in energy majors and AstraZeneca weighed on the index amid thin holiday trading. Weak crude prices dragged Shell and BP, while NatWest slipped despite profit growth. Gains in Unilever, Rolls-Royce and Pearson offered some support, but overall sentiment remained subdued after mixed earnings.
articles.excerpt: London’s FTSE 100 edged lower as losses in energy majors and AstraZeneca weighed on the index amid thin holiday trading. Weak crude prices dragged Shell and BP, while NatWest slipped despite profit growth. Gains in Unilever, Rolls-Royce and Pearson offered some support, but overall sentiment remained subdued after mixed earnings.

articles.title: Bank Lending to NBFCs Rises 26%, Fastest in FY26
articles.content: In the previous fiscal year, bank lending to Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) witnessed an impressive 26 percent increase, fueled by the Reserve Bank of India's easing of risk weights. This favorable shift, along with more lenient regulations and attractively lower lending rates, has propelled a surge in NBFC financing.
articles.excerpt: In the previous fiscal year, bank lending to Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) witnessed an impressive 26 percent increase, fueled by the Reserve Bank of India's easing of risk weights. This favorable shift, along with more lenient regulations and attractively lower lending rates, has propelled a surge in NBFC financing.

articles.title: Global markets tread water as yen moves dominate currency trading
articles.content: Global markets steadied as investors tracked sharp yen movements following suspected Japanese intervention. The dollar briefly slid before stabilising, while equities remained supported by strong tech earnings. Central banks held rates, but hawkish signals from the Fed and ECB kept markets cautious. Rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions added to inflation concerns.
articles.excerpt: Global markets steadied as investors tracked sharp yen movements following suspected Japanese intervention. The dollar briefly slid before stabilising, while equities remained supported by strong tech earnings. Central banks held rates, but hawkish signals from the Fed and ECB kept markets cautious. Rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions added to inflation concerns.

articles.title: Indian banks need to grow global forex game: RBI governor Sanjay Malhotra
articles.content: In a bold call to action, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra challenged Indian banks to step up as global market-makers for the rupee. This move aims to reclaim the offshore rupee trading space. He pointed out the current deficiencies in rupee derivatives and emphasized the recent tightening of foreign exchange market regulations to minimize currency fluctuations.
articles.excerpt: In a bold call to action, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra challenged Indian banks to step up as global market-makers for the rupee. This move aims to reclaim the offshore rupee trading space. He pointed out the current deficiencies in rupee derivatives and emphasized the recent tightening of foreign exchange market regulations to minimize currency fluctuations.

articles.title: RBI steps up play in us treasuries, other securities
articles.content: In a strategic pivot, the Reserve Bank of India has cut down its deposits with other central banks and is now channeling more resources into US Treasury bills. This decision underscores a growing preference for assets that hold global liquidity, prompted by rising geopolitical tensions. Notably, India's foreign exchange reserves have also been strengthened through this transition.
articles.excerpt: In a strategic pivot, the Reserve Bank of India has cut down its deposits with other central banks and is now channeling more resources into US Treasury bills. This decision underscores a growing preference for assets that hold global liquidity, prompted by rising geopolitical tensions. Notably, India's foreign exchange reserves have also been strengthened through this transition.

articles.title: AI agents becoming day traders, but gains prove elusive
articles.content: In a groundbreaking shift, retail investors are now harnessing artificial intelligence to facilitate their trading endeavors. Many see this as a revolutionary step in the investment landscape, with enthusiastic supporters touting the potential for significantly enhanced returns. Emerging platforms enable users to seamlessly integrate AI-driven models into their trading strategies through straightforward commands.
articles.excerpt: In a groundbreaking shift, retail investors are now harnessing artificial intelligence to facilitate their trading endeavors. Many see this as a revolutionary step in the investment landscape, with enthusiastic supporters touting the potential for significantly enhanced returns. Emerging platforms enable users to seamlessly integrate AI-driven models into their trading strategies through straightforward commands.

articles.title: FPIs bet small on local debt, inflow pickup seen far away
articles.content: April witnessed a slight inflow of foreign investments into Indian debt, but the atmosphere remains one of caution. Influences like escalating US and Japan interest rates, a weaker rupee, and fiscal uncertainties have created a challenging environment for investors. With the potential for continued outflows driven by oil prices, optimistic investment prospects appear dim in the short term.
articles.excerpt: April witnessed a slight inflow of foreign investments into Indian debt, but the atmosphere remains one of caution. Influences like escalating US and Japan interest rates, a weaker rupee, and fiscal uncertainties have created a challenging environment for investors. With the potential for continued outflows driven by oil prices, optimistic investment prospects appear dim in the short term.

articles.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: US stocks rally could find fuel in earnings, jobs data amid surging oil prices
articles.content: Indian markets are watching US stocks next week. Investors will focus on company earnings and job data. This comes as oil prices rise and the US Federal Reserve signals a tougher stance. Despite global tensions, US stocks have seen a strong rally. Upcoming reports will guide market direction.
articles.excerpt: Indian markets are watching US stocks next week. Investors will focus on company earnings and job data. This comes as oil prices rise and the US Federal Reserve signals a tougher stance. Despite global tensions, US stocks have seen a strong rally. Upcoming reports will guide market direction.

articles.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq end higher, notch weekly gains after earnings-heavy week
articles.content: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs, fueled by strong earnings and falling crude prices, marking their longest weekly gain streak since October 2024. Despite historical May weakness, analysts are optimistic about continued upward momentum, with Q1 earnings growth at 27.8% and 83% of companies beating estimates.
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs, fueled by strong earnings and falling crude prices, marking their longest weekly gain streak since October 2024. Despite historical May weakness, analysts are optimistic about continued upward momentum, with Q1 earnings growth at 27.8% and 83% of companies beating estimates.

articles.title: Zen Technologies Q4 results: Cons PAT falls 69% YoY to Rs 32 crore; revenue down 45%
articles.content: Zen Technologies saw a significant drop in its March quarter performance. Net profit declined by 69% year-on-year, while revenue from operations fell by 45%. The company's profit after tax is attributable to its shareholders. Zen Technologies has declared a final dividend of Re 1 per share for FY26.
articles.excerpt: Zen Technologies saw a significant drop in its March quarter performance. Net profit declined by 69% year-on-year, while revenue from operations fell by 45%. The company's profit after tax is attributable to its shareholders. Zen Technologies has declared a final dividend of Re 1 per share for FY26.

articles.title: Musk’s $158 billion payday is a lot like Tesla stock: full of hot air
articles.content: Elon Musk's massive $158 billion compensation package from Tesla highlights stark economic disparities. While workers face stagnant wages and job insecurity due to AI, Musk's pay, tied to future performance targets, dwarfs company earnings and reflects an unsustainable economic trend.
articles.excerpt: Elon Musk's massive $158 billion compensation package from Tesla highlights stark economic disparities. While workers face stagnant wages and job insecurity due to AI, Musk's pay, tied to future performance targets, dwarfs company earnings and reflects an unsustainable economic trend.

articles.title: US stock today | Spirit Airlines prepares to cease operations after bailout plan falls apart: Report
articles.content: Spirit Airlines is reportedly nearing operational shutdown after failing to secure a bailout plan involving bondholders and the U.S. government. Disagreements over financing terms and a sharp increase in jet fuel prices due to the war in Iran have derailed the discount carrier's recovery efforts.
articles.excerpt: Spirit Airlines is reportedly nearing operational shutdown after failing to secure a bailout plan involving bondholders and the U.S. government. Disagreements over financing terms and a sharp increase in jet fuel prices due to the war in Iran have derailed the discount carrier's recovery efforts.

articles.title: RBI should reject Tata Sons application to deregister as a CIC: InGovern Research
articles.content: Corporate governance firm InGovern Research Services urged the RBI to reject Tata Sons' application to deregister as a Systemically Important CIC. The firm argues that regulatory changes and Tata Sons' structural linkage to public funds via listed group entities make its bid untenable, advocating for an IPO by March 2027.
articles.excerpt: Corporate governance firm InGovern Research Services urged the RBI to reject Tata Sons' application to deregister as a Systemically Important CIC. The firm argues that regulatory changes and Tata Sons' structural linkage to public funds via listed group entities make its bid untenable, advocating for an IPO by March 2027.

articles.title: Sell in May and go away? Not so fast, as earnings, geopolitics may offset seasonal concerns
articles.content: Blindly following the old Wall Street adage "sell in ​May and go away" may prove costly, as investors weigh whether to call time on a powerful market recovery heading into a historically turbulent stretch of the year.
articles.excerpt: Blindly following the old Wall Street adage "sell in ​May and go away" may prove costly, as investors weigh whether to call time on a powerful market recovery heading into a historically turbulent stretch of the year.

articles.title: US stocks today: Roblox shares tumble as forecast cut signals safety measures weighing on user growth
articles.content: Roblox shares on Friday plunged to their lowest levels ​in 18 months after the company ​cut its annual bookings forecast, raising concerns that new safety ​measures could suppress user growth for several quarters.
articles.excerpt: Roblox shares on Friday plunged to their lowest levels ​in 18 months after the company ​cut its annual bookings forecast, raising concerns that new safety ​measures could suppress user growth for several quarters.

articles.title: US stocks today: Reddit rallies as AI‑driven ad growth fuels strong revenue outlook
articles.content: Reddit shares jumped ​over 12% on Friday after an ​upbeat quarterly revenue forecast underscored growing returns from the social ​media company's AI-driven advertising tools.
articles.excerpt: Reddit shares jumped ​over 12% on Friday after an ​upbeat quarterly revenue forecast underscored growing returns from the social ​media company's AI-driven advertising tools.
